inputs.text: Kubernetes is an open-source container orchestration platform that automates the deployment, scaling, and management of containerized applications. It was originally designed by Google and is now maintained by the Cloud Native Computing Foundation. Kubernetes provides a framework to run distributed systems resiliently, handling failover, scaling, and deployment patterns.

expectations.expected_response: Kubernetes is an open-source platform for automating container deployment, scaling, and management. Originally designed by Google, it is now maintained by the CNCF.

inputs.text: Machine learning operations, or MLOps, is a set of practices that aims to deploy and maintain machine learning models in production reliably and efficiently. It combines machine learning, DevOps, and data engineering. Key components include experiment tracking, model versioning, automated testing, and continuous integration and delivery for ML pipelines.

expectations.expected_response: MLOps combines machine learning, DevOps, and data engineering to deploy and maintain ML models reliably. Key components include experiment tracking, model versioning, and CI/CD for ML pipelines.

inputs.text: Docker is a platform that uses OS-level virtualization to deliver software in packages called containers. Containers bundle their own software, libraries and configuration files, and can communicate with each other through well-defined channels.

expectations.expected_response: Docker uses OS-level virtualization to package software into self-contained, communicating containers.

inputs.text: Retrieval-Augmented Generation combines information retrieval with text generation. A query is first used to retrieve relevant documents from a knowledge base using semantic similarity search with vector embeddings. These documents are then provided as context to a large language model, which generates a response grounded in the retrieved information, reducing hallucinations and improving factual accuracy.

expectations.expected_response: RAG combines information retrieval with text generation by using semantic search to find relevant documents. These documents provide context to an LLM, reducing hallucinations and improving accuracy.

inputs.text: Git is a distributed version control system designed to handle everything from small to very large projects with speed and efficiency. Every Git working directory is a full-fledged repository with complete history and full version-tracking abilities, independent of network access or a central server. Git was created by Linus Torvalds in 2005 for development of the Linux kernel.

expectations.expected_response: Git is a distributed version control system built for speed and efficiency across projects of all sizes. Each working directory contains a complete repository with full history, independent of network access. It was created by Linus Torvalds in 2005 for Linux kernel development.

inputs.text: PostgreSQL is a powerful, open-source object-relational database system with over 35 years of active development. It has earned a strong reputation for reliability, feature robustness, and performance. PostgreSQL supports both SQL and JSON querying, making it suitable for both relational and document-oriented workloads.

expectations.expected_response: PostgreSQL is a mature open-source database system known for reliability, robustness, and performance. It supports both SQL and JSON querying for relational and document workloads.

inputs.text: Prometheus is an open-source systems monitoring and alerting toolkit originally built at SoundCloud. It features a multi-dimensional data model with time series data identified by metric name and key-value pairs, a flexible query language called PromQL, and autonomous single server nodes that do not rely on distributed storage.

expectations.expected_response: Prometheus is an open-source monitoring toolkit featuring multi-dimensional time series data, PromQL queries, and autonomous server nodes.

inputs.text: Terraform is an infrastructure as code tool that lets you define both cloud and on-premise resources in human-readable configuration files. It can manage low-level components like compute, storage, and networking, as well as high-level components like DNS entries and SaaS features. Terraform uses a declarative approach where you define the desired state and it determines the steps to achieve it.

expectations.expected_response: Terraform is an infrastructure-as-code tool for defining cloud and on-premise resources in declarative configuration files. It manages everything from compute and storage to DNS and SaaS features.

inputs.text: Redis is an open-source, in-memory data structure store used as a database, cache, message broker, and streaming engine. It supports data structures such as strings, hashes, lists, sets, and sorted sets with range queries. Redis provides high availability via Redis Sentinel and automatic partitioning with Redis Cluster.

expectations.expected_response: Redis is an in-memory data store used as a database, cache, message broker, and streaming engine. It supports various data structures and provides high availability through Sentinel and Cluster.

inputs.text: Nginx is a web server that can also be used as a reverse proxy, load balancer, mail proxy, and HTTP cache. It was originally written by Igor Sysoev to solve the C10K problem of handling ten thousand concurrent connections efficiently.

expectations.expected_response: Nginx is a versatile web server and reverse proxy originally built to efficiently handle ten thousand concurrent connections.

inputs.text: Apache Kafka is a distributed event streaming platform used by thousands of companies for high-performance data pipelines, streaming analytics, data integration, and mission-critical applications. It combines messaging, storage, and stream processing to allow storage and analysis of both historical and real-time data. Kafka can handle trillions of events per day.

expectations.expected_response: Apache Kafka is a distributed event streaming platform for data pipelines, analytics, and integration. It combines messaging, storage, and stream processing to handle both historical and real-time data at massive scale.

inputs.text: GraphQL is a query language for APIs and a runtime for fulfilling those queries with existing data. It gives clients the power to ask for exactly what they need, making it easier to evolve APIs over time. Unlike REST, GraphQL lets you get all the data your app needs in a single request by specifying the data requirements in a structured query.

expectations.expected_response: GraphQL is an API query language that lets clients request exactly the data they need. Unlike REST, it can fulfill all data requirements in a single structured request.

inputs.text: FastAPI is a modern, fast web framework for building APIs with Python based on standard Python type hints. It is one of the fastest Python frameworks available, on par with NodeJS and Go. Key features include automatic interactive API documentation, editor support with autocompletion, and data validation using Pydantic. FastAPI was created by Sebastián Ramírez and released in 2018.

expectations.expected_response: FastAPI is a high-performance Python web framework for building APIs using standard type hints. It offers automatic documentation, editor autocompletion, and Pydantic-based data validation. Created by Sebastián Ramírez in 2018, it rivals NodeJS and Go in speed.

inputs.text: MinIO is a high-performance, S3-compatible object store built for large-scale AI and machine learning workloads. It runs on any cloud, on-premise hardware, or at the edge, and is designed to be lightweight and simple to deploy.

expectations.expected_response: MinIO is a high-performance, S3-compatible object store designed for AI/ML workloads that runs anywhere from cloud to edge.

inputs.text: Helm is the package manager for Kubernetes. It streamlines the installation and management of Kubernetes applications through packages called charts. A chart contains all the resource definitions necessary to run an application, tool, or service inside a Kubernetes cluster. Helm tracks releases so you can install, upgrade, and rollback applications easily.

expectations.expected_response: Helm is the Kubernetes package manager that uses charts to streamline application installation and management. It tracks releases for easy installation, upgrades, and rollbacks.

inputs.text: MLflow is an open-source platform for managing the end-to-end machine learning lifecycle. It provides tools for experiment tracking, reproducible runs, model packaging, and model deployment. MLflow integrates with popular ML libraries like TensorFlow, PyTorch, and scikit-learn, and supports a model registry for versioning and staging.

expectations.expected_response: MLflow is an open-source platform for managing the complete ML lifecycle including tracking, packaging, and deployment. It integrates with popular ML libraries and provides a model registry for versioning.

inputs.text: ArgoCD is a declarative, GitOps continuous delivery tool for Kubernetes. It automates the deployment of applications to specified target environments by monitoring Git repositories for changes and synchronizing the desired application state.

expectations.expected_response: ArgoCD is a GitOps CD tool that automates Kubernetes deployments by syncing application state from Git repositories.

inputs.text: n8n is a workflow automation platform that allows you to connect different services and build automations with a visual editor. It supports both self-hosted and cloud deployments, giving users full control over their data and workflows. With over 400 integrations and the ability to write custom code, n8n can automate virtually any workflow.

expectations.expected_response: n8n is a workflow automation platform with a visual editor supporting self-hosted and cloud deployments. With 400+ integrations and custom code support, it can automate virtually any workflow.

inputs.text: Ollama is a lightweight framework for running large language models locally on consumer hardware. It supports a wide range of open models including Llama, Mistral, and Qwen, with automatic GPU detection and optimization. Ollama exposes an OpenAI-compatible API, making it a drop-in replacement for cloud inference services.

expectations.expected_response: Ollama is a lightweight framework for running LLMs locally with automatic GPU optimization. It exposes an OpenAI-compatible API, serving as a drop-in replacement for cloud inference.

inputs.text: Pydantic is a data validation library for Python that uses type annotations to validate and serialize data. It provides fast, extensible validation with rich error messages and is the most widely used data validation library in the Python ecosystem.

expectations.expected_response: Pydantic is Python's most popular data validation library, using type annotations for fast validation and serialization with rich error messages.
